name: Apricot Muffins
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the apricots in the apple juice for 10 minutes.
2. In a bowl, combine the flour, baking powder, baking soda, ground cardamom or ginger, and chopped walnuts.
3. Beat together the vegetable oil, sugar, and egg in a separate bowl.
4. Add the soaked apricots with their juice and beaten egg mixture to the dry ingredients.
5. Mix until all the ingredients are just blended.
6. Spoon the batter into oiled muffin tins or paper muffin cups (sprayed with non-stick spray), filling them 3/4 full.
7. Bake in a preheated oven at 350°F for 10 to 15 minutes, or until golden brown.
Note: Each muffin contains 182 calories, 1 bread exchange, 1/2 fruit, 2 fat (22 grams carbohydrate, 4 grams protein, 10 grams fat), 97 mg sodium, and 166 mg potassium. Cholesterol is 34 mg. Source: Am. Diabetes Assoc. Holiday Cookbook by Betty Wedman, 1986. Suggestions: Use raisins if you do not have apricots. Leave out walnuts to reduce fat content, or use 2 egg whites instead of the whole egg to lower cholesterol if desired.
